summary: Joe Reis explains his motivation for extensive travel as a data industry speaker: curiosity about the world and desire to understand what practitioners are actually doing beyond what's shared online. He shares observations that AI has leveled the playing field for everyone and provides practical travel tips for frequent travelers.
key_points: Face-to-face conversations at conferences and with locals reveal insights that blog posts and social media cannot
AI has put everyone back at the starting line, creating unprecedented opportunities to invent new tools and ways of working
Different geographies respond differently to change - US more willing to experiment, Europe moves more slowly
The personal cost of travel is significant: jet lag, time away from family, physical exhaustion
money_quotes: AI has put everyone back at the starting line. I've rarely seen a more exciting time to create new ways of doing our work and craft.
Don't be a passive observer; be part of making this change happen. Invent new tools and new ways of working.
It's one thing to read blog posts, watch videos, and see rumors on social media. It's another thing to see things for yourself.
As Bill Inmon tells me, 'travelling like you do will make you old.' He's right.
key_insight: In a rapidly changing AI-driven landscape, direct human connection through travel provides irreplaceable insights that remote observation cannot match, making the personal costs worthwhile for those seeking to understand and shape the industry.
